docs.title: SCIENCE FLOW MODEL
docs.text:
Status: REQUIRES_HUMAN_REVIEW development diagnostic only NOT SEALED-TEST CERTIFIED not promotable

science_flow is the planned flow family for exploration work.

Planned lifecycle:

  • /research
  • /hypothesize
  • /design-experiment
  • /execute-experiment
  • /audit-experiment
  • /interpret
  • /iterate
  • /record-finding
  • /share

Flow outputs:

  • protocol artifacts
  • execution evidence
  • audit reports
  • finding records
  • share packets for PM context

Constraints:

  • Evidence levels and claim boundaries are required.
  • Share packets are context bridges, not promotion events.

docs.title: SCIENCE MONKEYS DOCTRINE
docs.text:
Status: REQUIRES_HUMAN_REVIEW development diagnostic only NOT SEALED-TEST CERTIFIED not promotable

Science Monkeys are the explore-side workflow team.

Role set:

  • Explorer GPT
  • Hypothesizer GPT
  • Designer GPT
  • Executor AI
  • Auditor GPT

Explore doctrine:

  • Explore uncertain knowledge before product exploitation.
  • Preserve failed and negative findings.
  • Keep protocol design and execution separated.
  • Keep interpretation and audit separated from execution.
  • /share passes audited context to PM, but /share does not equal promotion.

Human authority:

  • No AI may certify.
  • No AI may promote.
  • Human-controlled advancement is required.

docs.title: CODE FLOW MODEL
docs.text:
Status: REQUIRES_HUMAN_REVIEW development diagnostic only NOT SEALED-TEST CERTIFIED not promotable

code_flow is the planned flow family for product development work.

Planned lifecycle:

  • /dossier
  • /constitution
  • /specify
  • /clarify
  • /plan
  • /checklists
  • /tasks
  • /analyze
  • /implement
  • /execute
  • /audit

ContextBus status:

  • Current v0.2 broker capabilities remain unchanged.
  • Flow Core v0.3 is required before code flow commands are fully implemented.

docs.title: CODE MONKEYS DOCTRINE
docs.text:
Status: REQUIRES_HUMAN_REVIEW development diagnostic only NOT SEALED-TEST CERTIFIED not promotable

Code Monkeys are the exploit-side workflow team.

Role set:

  • PM GPT
  • Coder GPT
  • Helper/Codex AI
  • Auditor GPT
  • Human

Exploit doctrine:

  • Consume validated context through dossier/spec/plan workflows.
  • Separate planning, implementation, execution, and audit responsibilities.
  • Preserve evidence and governance gates.

Authority split:

  • PM: dossier, constitution, specify, plan
  • Coder: tasks, implementation
  • Helper/Codex: execution and evidence capture
  • Auditor: clarify, checklists, analyze, audit
  • Human: advancement and promotion decisions

docs.title: NanoResearch-Inspired Skill, Memory, and Preference Roadmap
docs.text:
Status:

REQUIRES_HUMAN_REVIEW development diagnostic only NOT SEALED-TEST CERTIFIED not promotable

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text:
This document records the post-share roadmap inspired by NanoResearch-style tri-level co-evolution: reusable procedural skills, durable memory, and preference adaptation.

It does not expand Science Monkeys v0.1 Routes 001. Routes 001 remains a command-route layer only.

docs.title: Doctrine
docs.text:
Build it the Arqon way:

No hidden memory as truth.
No ungated self-evolution.
No automatic paper/spec generation from weak evidence.
No preference learning without auditability.
No promotion without Human approval.

docs.title: Mapping
docs.text:
Skill Bank        → Arqon Skill Registry / Procedure Bank
Memory Module     → ContextBus user/project/flow/finding memory artifacts
Planner Policy    → governed, auditable planning preference records
Orchestrator      → ArqonMonkeyOS brokered flow orchestration
Research Pipeline → Science Monkeys + Code Monkeys lifecycle

docs.title: Future Milestones
docs.text:
1. Science Monkeys v0.1 Routes 001
  2. Science Monkeys v0.1 Share Integration 001
  3. Spec Kit / Code Monkeys handoff integration
  4. ContextBus Memory Model 001
  5. Skill Registry 001
  6. Preference Profile 001
  7. Governed Research Personalization 001

docs.title: Routes 001 Boundary
docs.text:
Routes 001 must preserve enough structure for future extraction:

  • role
  • phase
  • flow_id
  • artifact_type
  • parent references
  • evidence level
  • source refs
  • allowed claims
  • forbidden claims
  • failure mode
  • decision status

Routes 001 must not implement hidden personalization, policy learning, or autonomous research-paper generation.

docs.title: CyberGym Benchmark Track 001
docs.text:
Status:

REQUIRES_HUMAN_REVIEW development diagnostic only NOT SEALED-TEST CERTIFIED not promotable

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text:
Preserve a future benchmark track for testing Arqon Zero / MonkeyOS against CyberGym when the system is mature enough.

This is a roadmap document only. It does not activate CyberGym work now.

docs.title: Benchmark Goal
docs.text: Evaluate Arqon Zero / MonkeyOS on vulnerability reproduction tasks while measuring both task success and truth-governance quality.

docs.title: Why CyberGym Matters
docs.text:
CyberGym is useful because it stresses repository exploration, vulnerability understanding, proof-of-concept construction, test execution, false-success avoidance, and evidence discipline.

That aligns with the Arqon thesis that agentic work must be governed by proof and audit, not just generated answers.

docs.title: CyberGym Reproduction Score
docs.text: Did the system produce a working vulnerability reproduction?

docs.title: Truth-Governance Score
docs.text: Did the system avoid fake success, missing logs, skipped tests, hallucinated proof, promotion without evidence, and incomplete reproduction packets?

docs.title: Learning Score
docs.text: Does the system improve after repeated tasks through future Skill/Memory/Preference layers?

docs.title: Phase 1: CyberGym Lite
docs.text:
Run 20 to 50 selected tasks.

Measure baseline success, false-positive rate, time, cost, and audit quality.

docs.title: Phase 2: Cyber Prover Pipeline
docs.text:
Add a controlled pipeline:

Explore repo → identify target area → propose proof path → generate test → run test → minimize proof → audit → human review

docs.title: Phase 3: Compare Against Public Baselines
docs.text: Compare against public CyberGym baselines before making any MDASH-style comparison.

docs.title: Phase 4: MDASH-Style Comparison
docs.text: Only compare against MDASH-like claims when the same task split, scoring, budget, timeout, repo state, and proof criteria are available.

docs.title: Non-Claims
docs.text:
• no claim that Arqon beats MDASH
  • no production security claim
  • no certification claim
  • no autonomous exploit deployment
  • no unsafe operationalization

docs.text: REQUIRES_HUMAN_REVIEW development diagnostic only NOT SEALED-TEST CERTIFIED not promotable

docs.title: 00 — Arqon ContextOS Ground Truth
docs.text: Status: REQUIRES_HUMAN_REVIEW development diagnostic only NOT SEALED-TEST CERTIFIED not promotable

docs.title: One-sentence summary
docs.text: Arqon ContextOS is a repo-backed, Cloudflare-served, GitHub App-powered operating layer that lets PM, Coder, Auditor, Helper/Codex, and human operators share persistent project context, messages, notes, flows, Spec Kit-compatible artifacts, execution evidence, and gate decisions without manual copy/paste or daily context reconstruction.

docs.title: Core problem
docs.text:
The real problem is not that GPTs need files. The real problem is that every new GPT session starts without the working state of the project. The human has had to rebuild context every day by manually choosing which decisions, specs, reports, logs, policies, and current-state facts to paste into each AI.

ContextOS fixes this by making the repository the durable memory and Cloudflare the controlled broker. GPTs do not rely on chat memory. GPTs pull current state through role-bound Actions.

docs.title: Current architecture
docs.text:
PM / Coder / Auditor GPTs
  -> GPT Actions with role-specific bearer keys
  -> Cloudflare Worker broker
  -> GitHub App installation token
  -> private project repo such as ArqonZero
  -> generated context, notes, messages, future flows, and evidence artifacts

docs.title: Current repos
docs.text: Repo Current role ArqonContextOS Public infrastructure repo: Worker, OpenAPI, docs, schemas, templates ArqonZero Private project repo: generated context, messages, notes, future flows, evidence

docs.title: Current live broker
docs.text: https://arqon-contextos-broker.sonarum.workers.dev

docs.title: Current implemented capability
docs.text:
Working v0.2 capabilities:

  • /sync-context
  • /sync-constitution
  • /save-context
  • /send-message
  • /inbox
  • /open-message
  • /archive-message as safe copy-to-archive
  • PM/Coder/Auditor role isolation
  • private ArqonZero context loading through Cloudflare/GitHub App
  • note writes into ArqonZero through broker
  • message writes into ArqonZero through broker

docs.title: Current not-yet-implemented capability
docs.text:
Next capabilities:

  • /create-flow
  • /load-flow
  • /flow-status
  • /adv-flow
  • /write-flow
  • Spec Kit-aware commands: /dossier, /constitution, /specify, /clarify, /plan, /checklists, /tasks, /analyze, /implement, /execute, /audit
  • multi-user identity
  • multi-repo swarm generator
  • context rebuild after broker writes
  • GitHub Contents 409 retry hardening
  • claim/status/evidence hardening

docs.title: Correct abstraction
docs.text:
The old “run” model is superseded.

Use:

flow

not:

run

Reason:

A run sounds like one task or one execution. A flow can hold a sprint, milestone, phase group, audit point, Coder/Helper loop, Auditor gate, and human advancement decision.

docs.title: Command defaults
docs.text:
Commands must be short.

Normal use should not require:

  • project=ArqonZero
  • role=CODER_AI
  • a long flow_id
  • repeated /write-artifact calls after every GPT response

Defaults:

Field Default behavior project ArqonZero role inferred from broker key / GPT identity flow reference name alias preferred, flow_id optional flow type audit unless user specifies phase or milestone

docs.title: Human-friendly flow names
docs.text:
The name field in /create-flow is a short alias.

Example:

/create-flow type=audit name="flow00"

The system may create:

FLOW-2026-0001

but the human should usually load it with:

/load-flow name="flow00"

The broker must maintain a name-to-ID index and reject duplicate active aliases.

docs.title: Authority doctrine
docs.text:
PM specifies and plans. Coder tasks and implements. Helper executes and captures evidence. Auditor clarifies, analyzes, audits, and gates. Human advances and controls promotion.

No AI self-certifies. No AI auto-promotes. No AI silently changes another role’s authority.

docs.title: Natural-flow doctrine
docs.text:
Do not force every step through Auditor.

Fast path:

PM creates sprint/flow plan
Coder implements milestone chunk
Helper executes and reports
Coder repairs/iterates with Helper
Auditor receives evidence stream and audits at audit points
Human advances flow

Auditor is critical, but should be invoked at logical audit points, risk triggers, milestone boundaries, tripwire failures, claim/promotion requests, and high-risk subsystem boundaries.

docs.title: Spec Kit integration doctrine
docs.text:
ContextOS should not replace GitHub Spec Kit.

ContextOS wraps and governs it.

Spec Kit = specification-driven development lifecycle
ContextOS = role/team/repo/flow/artifact/message/evidence bus

docs.title: Required status
docs.text:
Every governance-sensitive artifact must preserve:

REQUIRES_HUMAN_REVIEW development diagnostic only NOT SEALED-TEST CERTIFIED not promotable

docs.title: 01 — Vision, Mission, and Objectives
docs.text: Status: REQUIRES_HUMAN_REVIEW development diagnostic only NOT SEALED-TEST CERTIFIED not promotable

docs.title: Vision
docs.text: Arqon ContextOS is the persistent operating layer for governed AI development teams. It turns isolated GPT sessions into role-bound project operators that can share current repo-backed context, communicate through controlled message boxes, save non-official notes, manage official flows, preserve evidence, and advance work through explicit human-governed gates.

docs.title: Long-term vision
docs.text:
A human should be able to instantiate a reusable AI team for any repo:

  • PM AI for constitution, dossier, specification, planning, milestones, and definitions of done
  • Coder AI for tasks, implementation bundles, tests, docs, and Helper packets
  • Helper/Codex for local execution, command runs, evidence capture, reports, commits, and pushes when authorized
  • Auditor AI for clarification, checklists, analysis, tripwire requirements, milestone audits, integrity gates, and claim review
  • Human for flow selection, advancement, exceptions, and final promotion authority

A new GPT session should load the current project state with one command and stop depending on manually pasted history.

docs.title: Mission
docs.text: Arqon ContextOS exists to make AI team workflows persistent, auditable, role-separated, multi-user, multi-repo, and specification-driven.

docs.title: Mission commitments
docs.text:
ContextOS must:

  • remove daily context reconstruction
  • preserve role boundaries
  • persist useful context without laundering notes into official artifacts
  • support official flow artifacts and gate decisions
  • integrate GitHub Spec Kit lifecycle artifacts
  • support multi-user operation without forcing every command to name the user
  • support multi-repo swarm replication
  • keep the human in control of advancement and promotion
  • make unsafe or deceptive workflows fail closed

docs.title: Objective 1 — Daily continuity
docs.text:
A user opens a GPT and runs:

/sync-context

The GPT knows current project state, active flows, inbox items, recent decisions, and role authority.

docs.title: Objective 2 — Shared but controlled memory
docs.text:
Use notes and messages for non-official saved context and communication.

Rules:

  • notes are informal
  • messages are informal
  • official artifacts must live in flows
  • PM must explicitly promote useful ideas into specs/plans

docs.title: Objective 3 — Official flow system
docs.text:
Implement and use:

  • /create-flow
  • /load-flow
  • /flow-status
  • /adv-flow
  • /write-flow

Flows replace runs at the user level.

docs.title: Objective 4 — Spec Kit-aware lifecycle
docs.text:
Support:

  • /dossier
  • /constitution
  • /specify
  • /clarify
  • /plan
  • /checklists
  • /tasks
  • /analyze
  • /implement
  • /execute
  • /audit

docs.title: Objective 5 — Natural flows, not bottlenecks
docs.text: Coder and Helper should iterate quickly. Auditor should receive evidence and gate at audit points, not slow every minor iteration.

docs.title: Objective 6 — Multi-user support
docs.text:
Both the user and partner must be able to use the same GPT team without every command requiring a user name.

Near-term:

  • record actor_role
  • optionally record actor_user_label
  • add /whoami or profile binding later

Long-term:

  • OAuth into the ContextOS broker
  • per-human identity and audit trail

docs.title: Objective 7 — Multi-repo swarms
docs.text:
A config file should define a repo-specific GPT team and generate setup packs for PM, Coder, Auditor, and Helper.

Example future file:

contextos.swarm.yaml

docs.title: Success definition
docs.text:
ContextOS succeeds when the user can:

  1. Start PM GPT and load current state.
  2. Create an audit/milestone/phase flow with a short name.
  3. Have PM generate Spec Kit-aware docs.
  4. Have Coder load the flow and produce tasks/implementation bundle.
  5. Have Helper execute in VS Code and write evidence.
  6. Have Auditor audit at milestone/risk points.
  7. Advance the flow through human-controlled gates.
  8. Repeat this across repos without rebuilding the GPT team from scratch.

docs.title: 02 — Current System Status
docs.text: Status: REQUIRES_HUMAN_REVIEW development diagnostic only NOT SEALED-TEST CERTIFIED not promotable

docs.title: Operational state
docs.text: ContextOS v0.2 is operational for context sync, non-official notes, and role messages.

docs.title: Proven working pieces
docs.text: Capability Status Cloudflare Worker health Working GitHub App private repo access Working ArqonZero project context generation Working PM/Coder/Auditor role keys Working Role isolation Working /sync-context Working /sync-constitution Working as context/constitution route /save-context Working /send-message Working /inbox Working /open-message Working /archive-message Working as safe copy-to-archive

docs.title: ArqonContextOS
docs.text:
Public infrastructure repo.

Owns:

  • Worker source
  • OpenAPI schema
  • docs
  • schemas
  • project templates
  • GPT command docs

docs.title: ArqonZero
docs.text:
Private project repo.

Owns:

  • generated role contexts
  • context manifest
  • notes
  • messages
  • future flows
  • evidence artifacts

docs.title: Live broker
docs.text: https://arqon-contextos-broker.sonarum.workers.dev

docs.title: Important OpenAPI rule
docs.text:
Use OpenAPI 3.0.3 for GPT Action compatibility.

Do not put bearer auth blocks in the schema.

Correct model:

OpenAPI schema = endpoint and parameter shape
GPT Builder UI = API Key / Bearer authentication

Do not paste Bearer <key> into GPT Builder.

Paste only the raw broker key.

docs.title: GitHub App current state
docs.text:
GitHub App is owned by novelbytelabs and installed on ArqonZero.

Needed permissions:

  • Metadata: read-only
  • Contents: read/write

Contents read/write is required for notes/messages and future flows.

Do not commit the GitHub App private key. Do not paste it into chat.

docs.title: Known limitations
docs.text: Limitation Status Flow commands not implemented next Spec Kit-aware artifact commands not implemented planned after Flow Core Multi-user identity not implemented planned Multi-repo swarm generator not implemented planned GitHub Contents 409 retry missing hardening needed Archive is safe copy, not true move/delete acceptable v0.2 Context rebuild after broker writes is incomplete hardening needed

docs.title: Do not regress
docs.text:
Do not reintroduce /create-run, /load-run, or /write-artifact as the primary user model.

User-facing official workflow commands should now be flow-based.

docs.title: 03 — Architecture
docs.text: Status: REQUIRES_HUMAN_REVIEW development diagnostic only NOT SEALED-TEST CERTIFIED not promotable

docs.title: System architecture
docs.text:
Human operator(s)
  -> PM / Coder / Auditor GPTs
  -> GPT Actions
  -> Cloudflare Worker broker
  -> GitHub App installation token
  -> project repo such as ArqonZero
  -> context, notes, messages, flows, artifacts, evidence

docs.title: GPTs
docs.text:
Role-bound user interfaces.

They do not own durable state. They load and write through ContextOS.

docs.title: Cloudflare Worker broker
docs.text:
Owns:

  • role authentication
  • project mapping
  • path allowlists
  • endpoint routing
  • GitHub App token usage
  • safe writes to governance paths
  • role isolation

docs.title: GitHub App
docs.text:
Owns scoped repo access.

The app should be installed only on approved repos in the security domain.

docs.title: Project repo
docs.text:
Owns durable state.

ArqonZero stores:

  • generated context
  • messages
  • notes
  • flows
  • official artifacts
  • evidence
  • decisions

docs.title: ArqonContextOS repo
docs.text: Owns reusable infrastructure and docs.

docs.title: Data object hierarchy
docs.text:
Project
  Context
  Users / team metadata
  Notes
  Messages
  Flows
    Flow manifest
    Spec Kit artifacts
    Role artifacts
    Evidence artifacts
    Gate state
    Human decisions

docs.title: Object classes
docs.text: Object Official? Purpose Context snapshot Yes, generated state Load current role context Note No Save idea/analysis/suggestion Message No Directed role-to-role communication Flow Yes Official workflow container Flow artifact Yes Official role work product Evidence manifest Yes Execution/audit evidence Human decision Yes Advancement, exception, promotion decision

docs.title: Path rules
docs.text:
Broker writes should be restricted to governance paths.

Allowed future roots:

  • governance/context/
  • governance/notes/
  • governance/messages/
  • governance/flows/
  • governance/ledger/

Forbidden roots/components:

  • .env
  • secrets/
  • sealed/
  • holdout/
  • models/
  • data/
  • private/
  • credentials/
  • private keys
  • broker keys

docs.title: Broker auth
docs.text:
Role keys identify role. Future OAuth/user identity identifies the human.

Near-term identity:

role = inferred from broker key
user = unknown/manual label

Target identity:

role = GPT/broker role
user = OAuth identity
repo/team = project config

docs.title: 04 — Role Authority Model
docs.text: Status: REQUIRES_HUMAN_REVIEW development diagnostic only NOT SEALED-TEST CERTIFIED not promotable

docs.title: Role summary
docs.text: Role Owns Must not do PM AI constitution, dossier, specification, plan, milestones, definitions of done implement, execute, audit, certify, promote Coder AI tasks, implementation bundle, tests/docs patch plan, Helper packet define final gates, audit, certify, promote Helper/Codex local execution, command runs, logs, evidence, authorized micro-edits design architecture, audit, plan, promote Auditor AI clarify, checklists, analyze, audit, tripwires, gate reports implement, relax gates, promote Human choose flow, approve advancement, authorize commit/push/promotion erase evidence or bypass required gates

docs.title: PM AI
docs.text:
Allowed commands:

  • /sync-context
  • /sync-constitution
  • /inbox
  • /save-context
  • /send-message
  • /create-flow
  • /load-flow
  • /flow-status
  • /adv-flow when carrying human instruction
  • /dossier
  • /constitution
  • /specify
  • /plan

Forbidden:

  • /tasks
  • /implement
  • /execute
  • /audit as final auditor
  • certifying or promoting

docs.title: Coder AI
docs.text:
Allowed commands:

  • /sync-context
  • /inbox
  • /save-context
  • /send-message
  • /load-flow
  • /flow-status
  • /tasks
  • /implement

Forbidden:

  • /constitution
  • /plan as PM authority
  • /audit
  • /adv-flow unless explicitly authorized as a non-gate action
  • promotion or certification

docs.title: Auditor AI
docs.text:
Allowed commands:

  • /sync-context
  • /inbox
  • /save-context
  • /send-message
  • /load-flow
  • /flow-status
  • /clarify
  • /checklists
  • /analyze
  • /audit

Forbidden:

  • implementation patches
  • Helper execution
  • PM final planning authority
  • promotion or certification

docs.title: Helper/Codex
docs.text:
Allowed:

  • load Helper packet
  • execute authorized commands
  • apply authorized patches/micro-edits
  • write execution report
  • write evidence manifest
  • commit/push only if explicitly authorized

Forbidden:

  • self-directed architecture changes
  • broad coding without Coder packet
  • audit verdicts
  • hidden helpful drift
  • unauthorized path mutation

docs.title: Human
docs.text:
Owns:

  • flow type decision
  • human approval or rejection
  • advancement through /adv-flow
  • commit/push authorization when needed
  • promotion/claim decisions

Human decisions should be recorded as artifacts, not implicit chat statements when the flow reaches a governance-sensitive gate.

docs.title: 05 — Command Model
docs.text: Status: REQUIRES_HUMAN_REVIEW development diagnostic only NOT SEALED-TEST CERTIFIED not promotable

docs.title: Command design principles
docs.text:
Commands must be short, role-aware, and defaulted.

Normal users should not need to provide:

  • project=ArqonZero
  • role=PM_AI
  • long flow IDs
  • separate artifact-write commands after every major command

docs.title: /sync-context
docs.text:
Loads current role context.

Preferred usage:

/sync-context

Explicit usage still allowed:

/sync-context project=ArqonZero role=PM_AI

docs.title: /sync-constitution
docs.text: Loads current role constitution/governance context.

docs.title: /save-context
docs.text:
Saves non-official context note.

Example:

/save-context title="Coder deception-resistance suggestions" tags=contextos,deception,governance

docs.title: /send-message
docs.text:
Sends non-official directed message.

Example:

/send-message to=PM_AI subject="Review saved suggestions"

The from role should be inferred from the broker key.

docs.title: /inbox
docs.text: Lists messages for the authenticated role.

docs.title: /open-message
docs.text: Opens a message by ID.

docs.title: /archive-message
docs.text: Archives a message. Current v0.2 behavior is safe copy-to-archive.

docs.title: /create-flow
docs.text:
Creates official flow container.

Preferred:

/create-flow type=audit name="flow00"

Rules:

  • PM GPT only
  • type not flow-type
  • name is required and human-friendly
  • project defaults to ArqonZero
  • role inferred
  • PM artifacts should be written automatically

docs.title: /load-flow
docs.text:
Loads flow by name or ID.

Preferred:

/load-flow name="flow00"

docs.title: /flow-status
docs.text: Shows stage, next actor, blockers, gate state, evidence state, and human action required.

docs.title: /adv-flow
docs.text:
Advances flow after criteria/human decision.

Preferred:

/adv-flow name="flow00" decision="milestone-1-approved"

docs.title: /write-flow
docs.text:
Manual/backend primitive for official artifact writes.

Users should rarely need this because role-specific commands should auto-write the proper artifacts.

docs.title: Planned Spec Kit-aware commands
docs.text:
PM:

  • /dossier
  • /constitution
  • /specify
  • /plan

Auditor:

  • /clarify
  • /checklists
  • /analyze
  • /audit

Coder:

  • /tasks
  • /implement

Helper:

  • /execute

docs.title: Command compatibility notes
docs.text:
The GPT Action OpenAPI schema should be GPT-compatible:

  • OpenAPI 3.0.3
  • no bearerAuth security block
  • bearer keys configured in GPT Builder UI
  • inbox endpoint path is /v1/messages/inbox
  • send message endpoint remains POST /v1/messages

docs.title: 06 — Flow Model
docs.text: Status: REQUIRES_HUMAN_REVIEW development diagnostic only NOT SEALED-TEST CERTIFIED not promotable

docs.title: Why flows replaced runs
docs.text:
The original /create-run, /load-run, and /write-artifact model was too narrow. The user’s real work is not a single task. It is a natural pipeline of planning, phases, milestones, Coder/Helper iterations, audits, and human advancement decisions.

Use flows instead of runs.

docs.title: Phase flow
docs.text:
Use when PM should stay closely involved after each phase.

Pattern:

PM plans phase
Coder implements
Helper executes
Coder repairs
PM advances or replans

docs.title: Milestone flow
docs.text:
Use when speed matters and PM has already planned the milestone chunk.

Pattern:

PM creates milestone plan
Coder implements phases in chunk
Helper iterates with Coder
Auditor only invoked if requested or triggered
PM receives completion report

docs.title: Audit flow
docs.text:
Use as safest default for governance, RSI, deception-sensitive, evidence-sensitive, and claim-sensitive work.

Pattern:

PM creates phased plan + audit points
Coder implements milestone chunk
Helper executes and reports to Coder + Auditor evidence stream
Auditor gates at milestone/audit point
Human advances or sends back for repair

docs.title: Flow aliases
docs.text:
Each flow has:

  • a canonical flow_id
  • a human-friendly name

Example:

name = flow00
flow_id = FLOW-2026-0001

Users should be able to load by either.

Duplicate active names must fail closed.

docs.title: Flow repository structure
docs.text:
Planned structure:

governance/flows/
  flow_index.json

  FLOW-2026-0001/
    flow_manifest.json

    contextos/
      flow_status.json
      gate_state.json
      human_decision.md

    speckit/
      dossier.md
      constitution.md
      spec.md
      clarification.md
      plan.md
      checklists.md
      tasks.md
      analysis.md

    artifacts/
      coder_implementation_bundle.md
      coder_handoff_to_helper.md
      helper_execution_report.md
      evidence_manifest.json
      auditor_audit_blueprint.md
      auditor_gate_report.md
      auditor_repair_request.md
      human_decision.md

    messages/

docs.title: Flow gate levels
docs.text:
Do not use a numeric auditor score as the primary advancement mechanism.

Use logical gates:

docs.title: Gate 0 — PLAN_READY
docs.text:
The plan is scoped enough to start.

Requires:

  • objective
  • phases
  • milestones
  • definition of done
  • allowed/forbidden paths
  • risks
  • audit points
  • gate criteria

docs.title: Gate 1 — DEV_EVIDENCE_READY
docs.text:
Coder + Helper produced runnable development evidence.

Requires:

  • Coder bundle
  • Helper report
  • command logs
  • exit codes
  • tests/results
  • no obvious forbidden path mutation

docs.title: Gate 2 — INTEGRITY_GATE_PASSED
docs.text:
Auditor found milestone evidence coherent enough to advance.

Requires:

  • audit report
  • evidence manifest
  • tripwire/guardrail checks
  • claim/evidence alignment
  • no unresolved deception/gaming hard fail

docs.title: Gate 3 — CLAIM_OR_PROMOTION_CANDIDATE
docs.text:
The result may support a stronger claim or promotion decision.

Requires:

  • strict audit
  • replayable evidence
  • sealed/holdout boundary respected if relevant
  • human review
  • promotion/claim manifest
  • no unresolved hard fails

Gate 3 still does not mean automatic promotion.

docs.title: 07 — Spec Kit Integration
docs.text: Status: REQUIRES_HUMAN_REVIEW development diagnostic only NOT SEALED-TEST CERTIFIED not promotable

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text:
GitHub Spec Kit provides the specification-driven development backbone. ContextOS provides the multi-agent, repo-backed, role-governed flow and artifact system.

The correct integration is:

Spec Kit owns the development lifecycle.
ContextOS owns the team, flow, artifact, identity, and governance bus.

docs.title: Full command map
docs.text: Command Source/concept Owner Purpose /dossier ContextOS custom PM collect facts before specification /constitution Spec Kit constitution PM governing principles /specify Spec Kit specify PM what/why requirements /clarify Spec Kit clarify Auditor resolve ambiguity /plan Spec Kit plan PM technical strategy, milestones, definitions /checklists Spec Kit optional checklists Auditor primarily, PM request allowed validate completeness/clarity/consistency /tasks Spec Kit tasks Coder task breakdown /analyze Spec Kit analyze Auditor cross-artifact consistency /implement Spec Kit implement Coder implementation bundle and Helper packet /execute ContextOS/Helper local execution Helper/Codex run commands/tests and capture evidence /audit ContextOS audit gate Auditor milestone/risk/claim audit

docs.title: /dossier
docs.text:
/dossier is custom and should happen before /specify when the project context is complex.

It gathers:

  • current repo context
  • recent messages
  • saved notes
  • current risks
  • open blockers
  • prior decisions
  • relevant docs
  • current user intent

docs.title: PM-owned Spec Kit stages
docs.text:
PM owns:

  • /constitution
  • /dossier
  • /specify
  • /plan

PM should generate Spec Kit-compatible artifacts, but must not claim to run local Spec Kit tooling unless Helper/Codex evidence confirms it.

docs.title: Auditor-owned Spec Kit stages
docs.text:
Auditor owns:

  • /clarify
  • /checklists
  • /analyze
  • /audit

Auditor should reduce ambiguity early and gate evidence at audit points.

docs.title: Coder-owned Spec Kit stages
docs.text:
Coder owns:

  • /tasks
  • /implement

Coder should load PM/Auditor artifacts and produce tasks, implementation bundle, tests/docs patch instructions, and Helper packet.

docs.title: Helper/Codex stage
docs.text:
Helper owns:

  • /execute

Helper executes locally in VS Code/Codex, captures evidence, and writes Helper reports.

docs.title: Artifact mapping
docs.text:
ContextOS flow paths:

governance/flows/<FLOW_ID>/speckit/constitution.md
governance/flows/<FLOW_ID>/speckit/dossier.md
governance/flows/<FLOW_ID>/speckit/spec.md
governance/flows/<FLOW_ID>/speckit/clarification.md
governance/flows/<FLOW_ID>/speckit/plan.md
governance/flows/<FLOW_ID>/speckit/checklists.md
governance/flows/<FLOW_ID>/speckit/tasks.md
governance/flows/<FLOW_ID>/speckit/analysis.md

Possible local Spec Kit bridge paths:

.specify/memory/constitution.md
specs/<feature>/spec.md
specs/<feature>/plan.md
specs/<feature>/tasks.md

docs.title: Integration doctrine
docs.text:
Do not let Spec Kit bypass ContextOS governance.

Do not let ContextOS bypass Spec Kit discipline.

The systems should reinforce each other.

docs.title: 08 — Multi-User and Multi-Repo Model
docs.text: Status: REQUIRES_HUMAN_REVIEW development diagnostic only NOT SEALED-TEST CERTIFIED not promotable

docs.title: Requirement
docs.text:
ContextOS must support both the user and partner using the same system and GPTs concurrently.

The system must identify who acted without forcing every command to include a user name.

ContextOS must also support replicating a PM/Coder/Auditor/Helper GPT team for additional repos.

docs.title: Current limitation
docs.text:
Current broker keys identify role, not human user.

Example:

BROKER_KEY_CODER -> authenticated role = CODER_AI

This does not identify whether Mike or a partner is operating the GPT.

docs.title: Target identity model
docs.text:
Long-term target:

GPT Action -> OAuth -> ContextOS Broker -> user_id + role + project

This lets the broker record:

  • actor_user_id
  • actor_display_name
  • actor_role
  • source_gpt
  • project
  • timestamp

without the user typing identity in every command.

docs.title: Near-term identity model
docs.text:
Until OAuth exists:

  • infer role from broker key
  • record actor_user_label: unknown or manual label
  • add /whoami later
  • avoid pretending user identity is cryptographically known

docs.title: Multi-user command rules
docs.text:
Normal commands should not require:

user=Mike

If the system cannot identify the user yet, the artifact should say so honestly.

docs.title: Multi-repo swarm requirement
docs.text:
The user wants to replicate the GPT team per repo.

A future repo config should define the swarm.

Proposed file:

contextos.swarm.yaml

Example contents:

swarm:
  name: ArqonZeroSwarm
  project: ArqonZero
  repo: novelbytelabs/ArqonZero

roles:
  pm:
    role: PM_AI
    gpt_name: Arqon Zero PM AI
    commands: [/sync-context, /create-flow, /dossier, /constitution, /specify, /plan]
  coder:
    role: CODER_AI
    gpt_name: Arqon Zero Coder AI
    commands: [/sync-context, /load-flow, /tasks, /implement]
  auditor:
    role: AUDITOR_AI
    gpt_name: Arqon Zero Auditor AI
    commands: [/sync-context, /load-flow, /clarify, /checklists, /analyze, /audit]
  helper:
    role: HELPER_CODEX
    name: VS Code Codex Helper
    commands: [/execute]

defaults:
  project_required_in_commands: false
  role_required_in_commands: false
  user_required_in_commands: false
  default_flow_type: audit

docs.title: Swarm generator target
docs.text:
Future tool:

contextos init-swarm --config contextos.swarm.yaml

It should generate:

  • PM GPT instruction pack
  • Coder GPT instruction pack
  • Auditor GPT instruction pack
  • Helper/Codex instruction pack
  • conversation starters
  • OpenAPI action setup notes
  • project context templates
  • install checklist

Near-term limitation:

GPT creation/editing still happens in GPT Builder manually. The generator should produce setup packs, not claim to auto-create GPTs unless an official supported API exists.

docs.title: 09 — Security and Governance
docs.text: Status: REQUIRES_HUMAN_REVIEW development diagnostic only NOT SEALED-TEST CERTIFIED not promotable

docs.title: Non-negotiables
docs.text:
• No AI self-certification.
  • No AI auto-promotion.
  • No hidden side channels.
  • No arbitrary repo read/write.
  • No sealed/holdout contamination.
  • No benchmark gaming.
  • No claim laundering.
  • No weakening of required status labels.
  • No secrets in docs, GPT Knowledge, chat, or repo files.

docs.title: Required status labels
docs.text:
Every governance-sensitive output must preserve:

REQUIRES_HUMAN_REVIEW development diagnostic only NOT SEALED-TEST CERTIFIED not promotable

docs.title: Secrets
docs.text:
Never commit or paste:

  • GitHub App private key
  • broker keys
  • Cloudflare tokens
  • installation tokens
  • personal access tokens
  • webhook secrets
  • client secrets

Safe-ish config values such as app IDs may be documented only when useful, but do not confuse them with secrets.

docs.title: Role boundary controls
docs.text:
Broker must enforce:

  • role inferred from bearer key
  • non-HUMAN roles can read only their own context/inbox unless explicitly authorized
  • Coder cannot read PM inbox
  • PM cannot read Coder context with PM key
  • Auditor cannot write implementation artifacts
  • Helper cannot write audit reports

docs.title: Notes/messages controls
docs.text:
Notes and messages must be marked:

official_artifact: false

They are not specs, audit reports, implementation bundles, or human decisions.

docs.title: Flow artifact controls
docs.text:
Official artifacts must be written into flow paths and must specify:

  • flow_id
  • flow name
  • artifact type
  • source role
  • actor identity where available
  • created_at
  • status labels
  • related gate or phase

docs.title: Audit gates
docs.text:
Use logical gate levels instead of single numeric score.

  • PLAN_READY
  • DEV_EVIDENCE_READY
  • INTEGRITY_GATE_PASSED
  • CLAIM_OR_PROMOTION_CANDIDATE

A gate pass is not production certification unless a separate human-controlled promotion process says so.

docs.title: Known hardening items
docs.text:
• GitHub Contents 409 retry with fresh SHA
  • hash-chain manifests for artifacts
  • claim-language guard
  • evidence completeness scoring
  • context rebuild after broker writes
  • flow advancement precondition checks
  • multi-user identity/OAuth
  • archive true move/delete safety

docs.title: 10 — Updated Roadmap, Plan, and Milestones
docs.text: Status: REQUIRES_HUMAN_REVIEW development diagnostic only NOT SEALED-TEST CERTIFIED not promotable

docs.title: Roadmap principle
docs.text:
Build in layers:

  1. Preserve the working v0.2 system.
  2. Add official flows.
  3. Make flows Spec Kit-aware.
  4. Add Helper/Codex execution bridge.
  5. Add multi-user identity.
  6. Add multi-repo swarm replication.
  7. Harden evidence and advancement.

Do not jump straight to full autonomy.

docs.title: Milestone 0 — ContextOS v0.2 Baseline Stabilized
docs.text:
Status:

Complete.

Includes:

  • Cloudflare Worker broker live
  • GitHub App connected to ArqonZero
  • PM/Coder/Auditor role keys working
  • /sync-context working
  • notes/messages working
  • role isolation tested
  • OpenAPI 3.0.3 compatibility understood

Exit criteria:

  • Do not regress any v0.2 endpoint.
  • Do not reintroduce invalid OpenAPI auth schema blocks.
  • Do not break role isolation.

docs.title: Milestone 1 — Ground Truth Documentation Consolidation
docs.text:
Status:

This bundle.

Purpose:

Merge older docs, user stories, newer design, roadmap, and Spec Kit/multi-user direction into one current ground truth.

Deliverables:

  • ground truth doc
  • V/M/O doc
  • architecture doc
  • role authority model
  • command model
  • flow model
  • Spec Kit integration doc
  • multi-user/multi-repo doc
  • security/governance doc
  • updated roadmap and milestones
  • implementation plan
  • runbook
  • user stories

Exit criteria:

  • docs reflect flows, not runs
  • docs include Spec Kit full command list including /checklists
  • docs include multi-user and swarm requirements
  • docs preserve natural-flow doctrine

docs.title: Milestone 2 — Flow Core v0.3
docs.text:
Purpose:

Implement official flow containers.

Commands:

  • /create-flow
  • /load-flow
  • /flow-status
  • /adv-flow
  • /write-flow

Endpoints:

  • POST /v1/flows
  • GET /v1/flows
  • GET /v1/flows/{flow_ref}
  • GET /v1/flows/{flow_ref}/status
  • POST /v1/flows/{flow_ref}/artifacts
  • POST /v1/flows/{flow_ref}/advance

Required features:

  • project default ArqonZero
  • role inferred from bearer key
  • name alias such as flow00
  • canonical flow ID
  • duplicate alias fail-closed
  • flow_index.json
  • flow_manifest.json
  • phase/milestone/audit flow types
  • basic gate state

Acceptance criteria:

  • PM can create flow00
  • Coder can load flow00
  • Auditor can load flow00
  • /flow-status name="flow00" works
  • unauthorized role actions fail closed
  • /adv-flow records advancement decision and checks minimum artifacts

docs.title: Milestone 3 — Spec Kit-Aware Flow Artifacts v0.4
docs.text:
Purpose:

Integrate Spec Kit lifecycle into flows.

Commands:

  • /dossier
  • /constitution
  • /specify
  • /clarify
  • /plan
  • /checklists
  • /tasks
  • /analyze
  • /implement
  • /execute
  • /audit

Artifact slots:

  • speckit/dossier.md
  • speckit/constitution.md
  • speckit/spec.md
  • speckit/clarification.md
  • speckit/plan.md
  • speckit/checklists.md
  • speckit/tasks.md
  • speckit/analysis.md
  • artifacts/coder_implementation_bundle.md
  • artifacts/coder_handoff_to_helper.md
  • artifacts/helper_execution_report.md
  • artifacts/evidence_manifest.json
  • artifacts/auditor_gate_report.md

Acceptance criteria:

  • PM can create dossier/spec/plan artifacts in a flow
  • Auditor can write clarification/checklists/analysis/audit artifacts
  • Coder can write tasks/implementation bundle
  • Helper execution packet is generated for VS Code/Codex
  • artifacts are role-bound

docs.title: Milestone 4 — Helper/Codex Execution Bridge v0.5
docs.text:
Purpose:

Make VS Code/Codex Helper read the repo, execute the latest flow packet, and report evidence.

Deliverables:

  • Helper execution prompt template
  • flow packet format
  • evidence manifest schema
  • command log convention
  • commit/push authorization fields
  • Coder+Helper iteration loop
  • Auditor evidence stream notification

Acceptance criteria:

  • Helper can resolve flow00
  • Helper reads Coder handoff
  • Helper executes authorized commands
  • Helper writes report and evidence manifest
  • Helper does not commit/push unless authorized
  • Helper report is available to Coder and Auditor

docs.title: Milestone 5 — Context and Broker Hardening v0.6
docs.text:
Purpose:

Strengthen reliability and integrity.

Deliverables:

  • retry-on-409 write logic
  • context rebuild after broker writes
  • archive true move/delete safety or explicit retained-copy semantics
  • artifact schema validation
  • status-label guard
  • claim-language guard
  • hash manifest
  • evidence completeness score

Acceptance criteria:

  • transient GitHub 409s handled safely
  • role artifacts validate before write
  • missing status labels fail closed
  • forbidden claims are flagged
  • flow context updates after writes

docs.title: Milestone 6 — Multi-User Identity v0.7
docs.text:
Purpose:

Support the user and partner using the same GPTs safely.

Near-term deliverables:

  • user metadata schema
  • actor_user_label field
  • /whoami draft command
  • explicit "identity unknown" fallback

Long-term deliverables:

  • OAuth into ContextOS broker
  • per-human user ID
  • team membership
  • per-user audit trail

Acceptance criteria:

  • artifacts record actor_role and actor_user field
  • system does not pretend to know identity when it does not
  • future OAuth path is documented

docs.title: Milestone 7 — Multi-Repo Swarm Generator v0.8
docs.text:
Purpose:

Replicate PM/Coder/Auditor/Helper team for any repo.

Deliverables:

  • contextos.swarm.yaml
  • generator script
  • PM GPT instruction pack
  • Coder GPT instruction pack
  • Auditor GPT instruction pack
  • Helper/Codex instruction pack
  • conversation starters
  • action setup checklist
  • repo integration template

Acceptance criteria:

  • new repo can be configured from a single file
  • generated docs include repo-specific variables
  • generated setup pack reduces manual GPT creation friction

docs.title: Milestone 8 — Governance Gate Maturity v0.9
docs.text:
Purpose:

Make flow advancement evidence-bound.

Deliverables:

  • gate criteria schemas
  • Plan Ready gate
  • Development Evidence Ready gate
  • Integrity Gate Passed gate
  • Claim/Promotion Candidate gate
  • human decision artifact
  • promotion/claim manifest template

Acceptance criteria:

  • /adv-flow refuses advancement when required artifacts are missing
  • high-risk gates require human decision
  • audit pass does not equal promotion

docs.title: Milestone 9 — ContextOS v1.0
docs.text:
Purpose:

Reusable, stable, multi-user, multi-repo, Spec Kit-aware AI team operating layer.

Success target:

A user can instantiate a repo team, sync context, create flows, use Spec Kit lifecycle artifacts, execute via Helper/Codex, audit at gates, advance by human decision, and preserve all artifacts in GitHub without daily context rebuilding.

docs.title: ROLE_MISMATCH
docs.text: The requested role does not match the authenticated GPT role. This is usually good.

docs.title: 403 from GPT Action but curl works
docs.text: Likely GPT Builder auth problem. Recreate action, set API Key → Bearer, paste raw role key only.

docs.title: GPT Builder schema crash
docs.text: Use OpenAPI 3.0.3. Do not include bearerAuth/securitySchemes in schema.

docs.title: GitHub 409
docs.text: Concurrent write conflict. Retry sequentially for now. Add v0.6 retry-on-409 hardening.

docs.title: Archive leaves inbox copy
docs.text: Expected v0.2 safe-copy behavior.

docs.title: 15 — User Stories and Acceptance Criteria
docs.text: Status: REQUIRES_HUMAN_REVIEW development diagnostic only NOT SEALED-TEST CERTIFIED not promotable

docs.title: Story 1 — Daily context continuity
docs.text:
As the human operator, I want each GPT to load current Arqon Zero state from the repo through a simple command, so I do not rebuild context manually every day.

Acceptance criteria:

  • PM loads PM context.
  • Coder loads Coder context.
  • Auditor loads Auditor context.
  • role isolation works.
  • repo state is durable memory.

docs.title: Story 2 — Human-orchestrated operation
docs.text:
As the human operator, I want to manually open and command each GPT while state persists through ContextOS, so I keep control without copy/paste overhead.

Acceptance criteria:

  • no always-on GPT push requirement.
  • Cloudflare stores/serves state.
  • GitHub stores artifacts.
  • human final authority preserved.

docs.title: Story 3 — Notes and messages are informal
docs.text:
As the human operator, I want Coder/Auditor suggestions saved and sent to PM without turning them into official artifacts.

Acceptance criteria:

  • /save-context marks official_artifact: false.
  • /send-message marks official_artifact: false.
  • PM must explicitly promote ideas into official specs/plans.

docs.title: Story 4 — Flows replace runs
docs.text:
As the human operator, I want named flows instead of isolated runs, so a sprint/milestone/audit sequence has one durable home.

Acceptance criteria:

  • /create-flow, /load-flow, /flow-status, /adv-flow exist.
  • flow holds PM, Coder, Helper, Auditor, and Human artifacts.
  • name alias maps to canonical flow ID.

docs.title: Story 5 — Short command defaults
docs.text:
As the human operator, I want commands to infer project and role, so I can type /load-flow name="flow00" instead of long boilerplate.

Acceptance criteria:

  • project defaults to ArqonZero.
  • role inferred from broker key.
  • conflicting role requests fail closed.

docs.title: Story 6 — PM sprint planning
docs.text:
As the human operator, I want PM GPT to turn vision/objectives into a flow-ready sprint with phases, milestones, definitions of done, and audit points.

Acceptance criteria:

  • PM creates dossier/spec/plan.
  • PM defines audit points.
  • PM defines gate criteria.
  • PM writes flow artifacts automatically.

docs.title: Story 7 — Coder + Helper fast iteration
docs.text:
As the human operator, I want Coder and Helper to iterate quickly while Auditor receives evidence for gates.

Acceptance criteria:

  • Coder loads flow.
  • Coder writes tasks/implementation/Helper packet.
  • Helper executes packet.
  • Helper report goes to Coder.
  • Helper evidence available to Auditor.

docs.title: Story 8 — Auditor at logical audit points
docs.text:
As the human operator, I want Auditor to gate milestones, claims, risky subsystems, and deception-sensitive work without slowing every small iteration.

Acceptance criteria:

  • PM plan includes audit points.
  • Auditor can clarify early.
  • Auditor can generate checklists.
  • Auditor can audit milestone evidence.
  • Auditor uses logical gates, not arbitrary score.

docs.title: Story 9 — Spec Kit-aware development
docs.text:
As the system designer, I want ContextOS flows to store Spec Kit-compatible artifacts, so the workflow is specification-driven and compatible with VS Code/Codex Spec Kit use.

Acceptance criteria:

  • /constitution, /specify, /plan, /tasks, /implement map to Spec Kit lifecycle.
  • /dossier, /execute, /audit complete the ContextOS-specific lifecycle.
  • artifacts stored in flow folders.

docs.title: Story 10 — Multi-user operation
docs.text:
As a team, both human users should operate the GPT swarm without specifying user identity in every command.

Acceptance criteria:

  • current role identity still works.
  • future user identity layer is planned.
  • artifacts record actor role and eventually actor user.

docs.title: Story 11 — Multi-repo swarms
docs.text:
As the system owner, I want to replicate the GPT team for any repo from a config file.

Acceptance criteria:

  • contextos.swarm.yaml defines repo/team variables.
  • setup packs can be generated.
  • commands and roles remain consistent across repos.

docs.title: Story 12 — Evidence-bound advancement
docs.text:
As the human operator, I want /adv-flow to advance only when required artifacts/gates exist.

Acceptance criteria:

  • missing evidence blocks advancement.
  • human decision recorded.
  • promotion-level advancement requires human authority.
  • audit pass does not equal promotion.

docs.title: Appendix — MonkeyOS doctrine expansion reference
docs.text:
The extended Natural Flow User Stories file now includes the MonkeyOS / ContextBus expansion with Science Monkeys and Code Monkeys story sections (25-38), including:

  • MonkeyOS umbrella-platform story
  • ContextBus infrastructure story
  • Science Monkeys explore lifecycle stories
  • Code Monkeys exploit lifecycle story
  • /share boundary and PM context bridge story
  • Mike/Ash multi-user identity story
  • Unified flow-family story (science_flow, code_flow, audit_flow, governance_flow)

Primary reference:

  • docs/04_flows_and_spec_kit/Arqon_ContextOS_Natural_Flow_User_Stories.md

Compatibility copies:

  • docs/Arqon_ContextOS_Natural_Flow_User_Stories.md
  • docs/project/specs/Arqon_ContextOS_Natural_Flow_User_Stories.md

docs.title: 16 — Glossary
docs.text: Status: REQUIRES_HUMAN_REVIEW development diagnostic only NOT SEALED-TEST CERTIFIED not promotable

docs.title: ContextOS
docs.text: Repo-backed operating layer for GPT team context, messages, notes, flows, artifacts, evidence, and governance.

docs.title: MonkeyOS
docs.text: Umbrella governed AI-team operating system platform.

docs.title: Arqon MonkeyOS
docs.text: Naming variant for MonkeyOS umbrella platform.

docs.title: ContextBus
docs.text: Infrastructure layer inside MonkeyOS; legacy name is ContextOS.

docs.title: ContextOS (legacy term)
docs.text: Legacy infrastructure naming retained for compatibility while rename is not yet authorized.

docs.title: Flow
docs.text: Official workflow container. Replaces run at the user level.

docs.title: Flow name
docs.text: Human-friendly alias such as flow00.

docs.title: Flow ID
docs.text: Canonical ID such as FLOW-2026-0001.

docs.title: Note
docs.text: Non-official saved context.

docs.title: Message
docs.text: Non-official directed communication.

docs.title: Artifact
docs.text: Official work product inside a flow.

docs.title: Spec Kit
docs.text: GitHub specification-driven development workflow/tooling.

docs.title: Dossier
docs.text: ContextOS pre-spec context artifact.

docs.title: Science Monkeys
docs.text: Explore-side team roles for uncertain knowledge work.

docs.title: Code Monkeys
docs.text: Exploit-side team roles for product implementation work.

docs.title: Explore
docs.text: Investigative lifecycle that develops and audits uncertain findings.

docs.title: Exploit
docs.text: Implementation lifecycle that turns validated findings into governed product work.

docs.title: Share Packet
docs.text: Bridge artifact carrying science findings into PM context with claim/audit boundaries.

docs.title: Finding Record
docs.text: Official science artifact preserving findings, evidence level, and claim boundaries.

docs.title: science_flow
docs.text: Generic flow family for exploration workflows.

docs.title: code_flow
docs.text: Generic flow family for product development workflows.

docs.title: governance_flow
docs.text: Generic flow family for doctrine/policy/process changes.

docs.title: Mike/Ash human identity distinction
docs.text: Human identity differs from role identity; temporary user labels may be used before OAuth.

docs.title: Gate
docs.text: Logical approval level or advancement condition.

docs.title: Phase flow
docs.text: Flow where PM remains closely involved.

docs.title: Milestone flow
docs.text: Flow where Coder/Helper work through PM-defined milestone chunk.

docs.title: Audit flow
docs.text: Flow where Auditor gates milestone/risk/claim points.

docs.title: Helper/Codex
docs.text: Local VS Code/Codex execution worker.

docs.title: Advancement
docs.text: Movement from one flow state to another, usually through /adv-flow.

docs.title: 17 MONKEYOS CONTEXTBUS DOCTRINE
docs.text: Status: REQUIRES_HUMAN_REVIEW development diagnostic only NOT SEALED-TEST CERTIFIED not promotable

docs.title: Definitions
docs.text:
• MonkeyOS / Arqon MonkeyOS: umbrella governed AI-team operating platform.
  • ContextBus: repo-backed infrastructure layer formerly called ContextOS.
  • Science Monkeys: explore workflow focused on uncertain knowledge and hypothesis discovery.
  • Code Monkeys: exploit workflow focused on building products from validated knowledge.
  • Arqon Zero: first project/product using the platform.

“MonkeyOS is the governed AI-team operating system. ContextBus is its repo-backed infrastructure layer. Science Monkeys explore uncertain knowledge. Code Monkeys exploit validated knowledge into products. Arqon Zero is the first project running on the platform.”

docs.title: Explore vs Exploit
docs.text:
• Science Monkeys explore truth.
  • ContextBus preserves and governs truth.
  • Code Monkeys exploit validated truth.
  • Human operators control advancement and promotion.

docs.title: Science Monkeys Roles
docs.text:
• Explorer GPT
  • Hypothesizer GPT
  • Designer GPT
  • Executor AI
  • Auditor GPT

docs.title: Code Monkeys Roles
docs.text:
• PM GPT
  • Coder GPT
  • Helper/Codex AI
  • Auditor GPT
  • Human

docs.title: Governance Constraints
docs.text:
• No AI may certify.
  • No AI may promote.
  • Human authority is required for advancement and promotion decisions.

docs.title: Bridge Rule
docs.text:
• /share is the bridge from Science Monkeys outputs into PM context.
  • /share does not equal promotion.

docs.title: 18 RENAME READINESS PLAN
docs.text: Status: REQUIRES_HUMAN_REVIEW development diagnostic only NOT SEALED-TEST CERTIFIED not promotable

docs.text:
• Repo rename is NOT authorized yet.

Before any rename proposal is approved, inventory all references to:

  • ContextOS
  • ArqonContextOS

docs.title: Required Review Scope

Review and map rename impact across:

  • README
  • docs
  • ground-truth docs
  • OpenAPI
  • Worker source
  • schemas
  • project templates
  • GitHub workflows
  • GitHub Pages/docs site
  • GPT Action URLs
  • Cloudflare Worker routes/deployment references

docs.title: Compatibility Language Requirement
docs.text:
All transition documents must preserve this compatibility sentence:

  • “ContextOS is the legacy name for the infrastructure now called ContextBus inside MonkeyOS.”

docs.title: Rollback and Quarantine Requirements
docs.text:
• Maintain a rollback plan for docs, URLs, and naming layers.
  • Quarantine partial rename branches until full impact review is complete.
  • Do not merge rename-path changes without explicit human approval.

docs.title: Human Approval Gate
docs.text:
• Human approval is required before any actual repo rename.
  • Human approval is required before changing public endpoint names or route paths.
